task_completion.description: Verify that the bcWebCam application is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main window of the bcWebCam application is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Options dialog is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A separate Options or Settings dialog window for bcWebCam is visible, typically accessed via the gear icon in the bottom toolbar.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Suffix' field is located within the Options dialog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text input field explicitly labeled 'Suffix' is visible in the Options dialog.

task_completion.description: Verify that the correct string is entered into the Suffix field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text field labeled 'Suffix' contains exactly the characters `|;` (a pipe character followed by a semicolon).

task_completion.description: Verify that the OK button is clicked to apply the changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The mouse cursor is seen clicking the 'OK' button in the Options dialog, or the dialog is successfully closed in the final screenshots after the correct suffix was entered.

integrity.description: Check that the agent used the actual bcWebCam GUI to configure the setting.
integrity.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam application interface is used. There are no terminal windows, PowerShell scripts, or registry editors used to bypass the graphical interface.

integrity.description: Check that no other settings were modified in the Options dialog.
integrity.visual_evidence: Other fields in the Options dialog (such as 'Prefix' or camera selection) remain unaltered by the agent during the trajectory.

integrity.description: Check that the agent didn't just type the expected answer into a text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The string `|;` is only typed into the bcWebCam Suffix field, not into Notepad, Wordpad, or any other text application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected suffix to be entered into the bcWebCam Options dialog is exactly `|;` (a pipe character followed by a semicolon).
